headline: Aerospace Supplier Arxis Eyes $11.2B IPO, Opening Cloud Infrastructure Deal Window
summary: Arxis, an aerospace parts manufacturer, is targeting an $11.2 billion valuation in a US IPO, signaling renewed investor appetite for deep-tech industrial players. Post-IPO capital typically accelerates digital transformation spend as newly public manufacturers modernize supply chain, MRO, and simulation workloads. For cloud providers, IPO-stage industrials represent a greenfield opportunity before hyperscaler incumbency is established.
oci_implication: Arxis and peers in the aerospace manufacturing vertical are prime targets for OCI's HPC and engineering simulation capabilities—an area where OCI can compete directly against AWS and Azure on price and performance before GSI relationships solidify post-IPO. Engaging ISV partners in the aerospace CAD/CAM and digital twin space now positions OCI to be embedded in Arxis's modernization roadmap while the organization is most receptive to infrastructure decisions.
